premise: I spent my $45,000 pension and twenty years writing a memoir of my late husband’s heroism, trusting my son-in-law’s small publishing house to handle it — he forged a copyright assignment, sold the film rights for $320,000 to a streamer, and released a ghostwritten version calling me a fabricator. I proved the forgery, reverted the rights, self-published the true story with a blistering foreword about his fraud, and when it sold 200,000 copies and earned $1.2 million, I bought his company’s debt and now own the imprint, with his name permanently banned.
